---
title: "Project Draft workshop"
author: "Joseph G., Barbara S."
date: "2026-04-08"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```




```{r}
library(tidyr)
library(readr)
my_data <- read.csv("imdb_top_1000.csv")

glimpse(my_data)
```
```{r}
# What are the top 5 directors with the the highest movie rating?

library(dplyr)

 top_5_directors <- my_data %>% # here we rename the data set to the top 5 ones 
   group_by(Director)%>%        # here we have to group by the directors coz this is what we want 
   summarise(avg_rating = mean(IMDB_Rating, na.rm = TRUE))%>% # then we have to summarize to the next variable that is the avg rating for the top 5 
   arrange(desc(avg_rating))%>% # here we have to arrange the avg rating data in descending order 
   head(5)                     # here we select the top whatever, like top 5 or top 10
 print(top_5_directors)         # here we print what the data set will look like 
 
 library(ggplot2)               # here we have to open the plot library to make the plot 
 
p <- ggplot(top_5_directors, aes(x = Director, y = avg_rating, fill = Director)) + geom_col() + labs(title = "TOP 5 DIRECTORS BY AVERGAE RATING", x= "Director's name", y = "IMDB Average rating")        # at the beginning we make it p (plot) <- ggplot that way it can be used for the plotly tool  

library(plotly)                 # here we open the plotly tool from the library 
ggplotly(p)                     # and lastly here we apply the plotly tool to "p"
```
1. What is the relationship between ratings and runtime?

```{r}

my_data <- my_data%>%
  mutate(Runtime = as.numeric(gsub(" min", "", Runtime)))
  
ggplot(my_data, aes(x = Runtime, y = IMDB_Rating)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  labs(
    title = "People's Rating vs Runtime",
    x = "Runtime",
    y = "IMDb Rating"
  )
```



```{r}
ggplot(my_data, aes(x = Runtime, y = Meta_score)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  labs(
    title = "Critics Score vs Runtime",
    x = "Runtime",
    y = "Meta_score"
  )
```
```{r}
# Who are the top 5 lead actors among the top imdp movies and how many times they appear in this data set ranking. 

library(dplyr)
top_actors <- my_data%>%
  group_by(Star1)%>%
  summarise(Movie_count = n())%>%
  arrange(desc(Movie_count))%>%
  head(10)
print(top_actors)

library(ggplot2)
g <- ggplot(top_actors, aes(x = Star1, y = Movie_count, fill = Star1)) + geom_col() +labs(title = "Top 5 lead actors and how many time they appear in the data set", x = "Lead actor's name", y = "Amount of times they appear in the dataset" )
library(plotly)
ggplotly(g)
```

2.	Which genres tend to have the longest runtime?

```{r}
my_data <- my_data %>%
  mutate(Runtime = as.numeric(gsub("[^0-9]", "", Runtime)))

my_data_genre <- my_data %>%
  separate_rows(Genre, sep = ", ")

genre_runtime <- my_data_genre %>%
  group_by(Genre) %>%
  summarize(avg_runtime = mean(Runtime, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  arrange(desc(avg_runtime))

head(genre_runtime)
```


```{r}
ggplot(genre_runtime, aes(x = reorder(Genre, avg_runtime), y = avg_runtime)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Average Runtime by Genre",
    x = "Genre",
    y = "Average Runtime (minutes)"
  )
```
